Social Media Ambassador

School of Visual Arts — Admissions

Produced short-form video content from creative concept and scripting to on-camera hosting and final editing. Worked closely with marketing directors to develop content informed by platform trends and audience engagement, while supporting live video production at major campus events. Utilized storytelling and editing techniques to create content that strengthened the School of Visual Arts' online presence and connected with prospective students.

Personal

Creative Independent Content Creator

YouTube & Instagram

Manage and grow personal YouTube and Instagram platforms through art- and life-focused content that combines storytelling, visual documentation, and audience engagement. Oversee the full content production process, including concept development, scripting, photography, filming, editing, thumbnail design, and platform promotion. Through consistent content creation, have produced over 40+ long-form videos generating 180,000+ views, along with short-form content that documents daily life, creative projects, and experiments with social media trends while building an engaged online community.

Event Coordination

Markets, parties, workshops, and gatherings.

Events that bring people together — and the coordination that makes it happen.

Worked as a paid Event Coordinator for the Visual Art Student Association, planning and executing large-scale events including 80-vendor markets and 600+ person parties. Concurrently served as Vice President of the Taiwanese Student Association, managing cultural events that brought together 100+ students across NYC universities.

About Me

Hi, I'm Christine.

Christine Lin is a New York-based creative — content producer and event coordinator.

She manages the full content production process across YouTube and Instagram, handling everything from concept development and scripting to filming, editing, thumbnail design, and platform strategy, growing her personal channels to 180,000+ views. Her professional work spans social media content for galleries, organizations, and universities, where she produces reels, documents events, and creates posts that maintain consistent, on-brand visuals across every platform.

Christine brings hands-on experience in event coordination, from 80-vendor markets and hands-on workshops, to 600-person Halloween parties and cross-university cultural gatherings uniting 100+ Taiwanese students across NYC.

Find me on
Instagram YouTube LinkedIn

Contributions
  • Reviewed and categorized 150+ vendor applications, assessing special requirements for each
  • Arranged seating charts to organize vendor placement based on needs and space
  • Went through the vendor selection process

  • Managed guest flow throughout the market to ensure a smooth experience
  • Promoted the event across social media to drive attendance and community engagement

  • Managed staff scheduling and event-day logistics to ensure smooth event-day operations

Workshop

Sockie Creature Workshop

Venue 📍 School of Visual Arts, New York
Date 📅 2025
Attendees 👥 30+ people

A 2-hour hands-on workshop guiding participants through building their own Sockie Dolls, encouraging creativity, craftsmanship, and community engagement.

Poster, photography, video by Christine Lin (me)
Contributions
  • Led the event from concept to execution — budgeting, material sourcing, and logistics
  • Designed promotional poster and handled event photography
  • Taught and guided 30+ participants through the sewing process, many with zero prior experience
  • Managed participant flow and adapted instruction in real time to accommodate all skill levels

Themed Party

Be My Valentine Baby

Venue 📍 School of Visual Arts, New York
Date 📅 2026
Attendees 👥 90+ people

A Valentine's Day craft event for the SVA community, featuring three hands-on sessions — card decorating, buddy-doll building, and pipe cleaner creature making — in collaboration with Student Engagement.

Photo & video gallery
View on Instagram ↗
Contributions
  • Co-led the event from planning to execution, organizing the schedule across all three craft sessions
  • Set up and arranged the event space to accommodate multiple concurrent activity stations
  • Communicated with staff throughout the event to ensure smooth run-of-show
  • Collaborated with the Student Engagement office
